title: senecio vulgaris subsp. viscosus
intro: Senecio vulgaris subsp. viscosus, also known as sticky groundsel, is a plant species within the dandelion family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone content. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, sticky groundsel is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential toxicity and side effects.
